immutable_core.semantic_intent: ICU/MCU admission baseline grouped proxy body weight
immutable_core.semantic_definition: A one-row-per-ICU/MCU local admission baseline body-weight proxy in kilograms, primarily mapped from source grouped weight buckets and explicitly labeled as proxy/binned rather than exact continuous measured weight.
immutable_core.semantic_grain: one row per anchor-qualified summary target
immutable_core.same_name_cross_database_rule: Any database asset published as std_weight_icu_baseline_grouped_proxy must preserve ICU/MCU admission baseline, grouped/binned source semantics, explicit proxy mapping, and provenance flags. It must not be treated as exact continuous measured body weight.

summary_contract.window_start_rule: use the source admission context for grouped weight; event fallback rows must be inside the approved near-admission +/-360 minute window
summary_contract.window_end_rule: grouped admission context has no event timestamp; event fallback rows must remain inside +/-360 minutes relative to local admission
summary_contract.qualifying_row_rule: retain one local admission row when an admissions_core.weightgroup bucket exists, or when a kept near-admission std_weight_event row exists inside the repair window; retain unresolved rows with null value when neither source is available
summary_contract.selection_rule: prefer admissions_core.weightgroup proxy; use std_weight_event fallback only when weightgroup is missing
summary_contract.tie_break_rule: for event fallback only, choose smallest absolute time distance, then fallback item priority, then relative time, then stable source_measurement_id
summary_contract.no_source_row_action: retain the local admission row with null std_weight_kg_baseline and unresolved provenance fields when no grouped or fallback source exists
summary_contract.partial_window_action: not_applicable_for_grouped_admission_context; event fallback remains bounded by the explicit +/-360 minute repair window

canonical_representation.range_role_note: These ranges govern retained proxy values and conservative event fallback values. Open-boundary groups are not hidden extrapolations; they remain boundary proxy values with representation flags.

external_alignment.alignment_policy: Use external terminologies as semantic anchors when reliable, but do not make them the backbone of the standard-variable system.
external_alignment.public_anchor_status: broader body-weight observation anchors exist, but grouped/proxy ICU-MCU baseline semantics are governed by this internal source-representation contract
external_alignment.note: The external body-weight concept anchors the clinical domain only. The grouped proxy representation, ICU/MCU admission context, and unresolved-row policy are internal contract semantics.

new_variable_trigger_examples: changing this variable from grouped/proxy weight to exact continuous measured weight
changing the anchor from ICU/MCU admission context to hospital admission context
version_bump_trigger_examples: adding a new database with the same grouped/proxy admission-baseline semantics
tightening the fallback-event repair rule without changing grouped-proxy primary semantics

allowed_database_specific_variation: raw bucket labels and local grouped weight coding
local source-context labels for measured, estimated, or anamnestic/asked weight source
database-specific local structural keys before normalization
presence or absence of repair-only event fallback
disallowed_same_name_variation: publishing exact continuous measured ICU baseline weight under this grouped/proxy variable
publishing hospital-admission baseline weight under this ICU/MCU admission proxy variable

execution_governance.formal_output_rule: Formal std_weight_icu_baseline_grouped_proxy outputs should go through execution.py reading this variable_spec and an approved mapping_spec. Execute-mode runtime evidence should record both spec hashes and formal output-file signatures.
execution_governance.future_non_bypassable_rule: Formal outputs should be generated only through the governed execution.py path reading this variable_spec and an approved mapping_spec.
